suppress.notes:
CVE-2026-0994 on com.google.protobuf:protobuf-java is a FALSE POSITIVE (see audit/F-deps-2.md).
1. Wrong ecosystem. CVE-2026-0994 is a Python-only DoS: a recursion-depth bypass in google.protobuf.json_format.ParseDict() when parsing nested `Any` messages. The authoritative GitHub advisory (GHSA-7gcm-g887-7qv7) lists ONLY the PyPI `protobuf` package (affected <= 6.33.4, fixed 6.33.5 / 5.29.6); it does NOT list the Maven com.google.protobuf:protobuf-java artifact.
2. Version-scheme CPE mismatch. NVD models the CVE as cpe:2.3:a:google:protobuf:* with versionEndIncluding "33.4" — protobuf's UNIFIED/Python numbering. dependency-check compares protobuf-java's Java version "4.34.2" against "33.4" and, because 4 < 33, wrongly matches it. In unified numbering protobuf-java 4.34.2 is train 34.2, NEWER than the affected <= 33.4 line. Upgrading is futile: every protobuf-java 4.x mis-compares as < 33.4.
3. Unreachable path anyway. The only protobuf use this session is the CP<->Gateway Handshake over BINARY protobuf on a loopback, dev-only plane parsing TRUSTED input; the messages contain no `Any` fields and no JsonFormat/JSON parsing is used, so the vulnerable json_format path is unreachable regardless.
Scope: narrowly limited to this one CVE on this one artifact. Any future, genuinely Java-affecting protobuf CVE has a different id and is NOT suppressed. Review by 2026-10-09 (or when protobuf-java's NVD CPE is corrected to Java numbering).
